meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Overcome project abandonment with this beginner-friendly checklist. Master user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your first real-world app.
content.answer_block: You struggle to finish projects because you lose momentum after the initial coding high. By implementing a structured user onboarding flow early, you create a tangible 'success state' for your users. This milestone-driven approach keeps you accountable, validates your features, and turns a messy prototype into a polished, launchable product.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the initial spark of an app idea is electric, the repository is initialized, and the first few commits feel like genius. But then, the complexity creeps in. The code becomes a labyrinth of half-finished features, and the 'shipping' finish line retreats into the fog of endless refactoring. You aren't failing; you are simply building without a destination.

User onboarding is the secret weapon of the seasoned engineer. It forces you to define exactly what your user needs to achieve to derive value. When you build the onboarding first, you build the 'North Star' of your application. This checklist will guide you through transforming your scattered code into a cohesive, user-centric product using the latest AI-assisted development tools.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the single action that delivers value to the user. This prevents feature creep by focusing your development on the critical path.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my app idea and define the shortest user journey from signup to the first 'Aha!' moment. Create a step-by-step user onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Document the onboarding journey in a new file named ONBOARDING.md'

steps.title: Skeleton UI Implementation
steps.description: Build the UI shells for your onboarding screens. Don't worry about logic yet; focus on the layout and the flow of information.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate React components for a 3-step onboarding wizard. Include placeholders for user data collection and progress indicators.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create components in /src/components/onboarding/ with basic Tailwind styling'

steps.title: State Management Setup
steps.description: Connect your onboarding steps to a global state to track user progress. This ensures the user doesn't lose their data if they refresh.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a simple React context or hook-based state machine to manage the onboarding wizard steps and user input storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create an OnboardingProvider and a hook to track current step index'

steps.title: Data Persistence Layer
steps.description: Ensure that once a user completes the onboarding, their profile is saved to your backend. This transforms a demo into a functional app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service function to save the onboarding data to a Supabase or Firebase database instance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a data service to persist the onboarding state to the database'

steps.title: Final Polish & Validation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to audit your onboarding flow for edge cases and ensure a seamless transition to the main dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the onboarding code for potential bugs, empty states, and UX improvements. Suggest a final 'success' state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Run a code review on the onboarding module and suggest 3 improvements for performance'

pro_tips: Always build the 'Happy Path' first; edge cases are the primary reason students quit mid-project.
Use 'Feature Flag' comments in your code to toggle onboarding features on/off during development.
Limit your onboarding to 3 steps maximum; if it takes longer, your core value proposition is too complex.
Record your screen while testing the onboarding to catch friction points you don't notice while coding.
Treat your onboarding documentation as a living contract that prevents you from adding unnecessary 'nice-to-have' features.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify to get your first external user feedback. Use the data collected to prioritize your next feature set.
content.conversion_block: Don't let your code gather dust in a private repository. Master the art of shipping by focusing on the user experience first. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
